description: Tailwind's default spacing scale is based on a 4px (0.25rem) base unit. Scale keys are numeric and non-linear, covering fine-grained small values through large layout spacings. Used for padding, margin, gap, width, height, and more.

Material Design uses an 8px base grid unit for spacing. All spacing values are multiples of 8px, ensuring visual consistency across components. Some fine-grained values (4px) are permitted for internal component spacing. The system is used in MUI via the theme.spacing() function.

Bootstrap 5 uses a 6-step spacing scale (0–5) built on a 4px base unit (1rem = 16px, $spacer = 1rem). Steps grow non-linearly: 0, .25rem, .5rem, 1rem, 1.5rem, 3rem. These are used in utility classes like p-3, mt-2, gap-4, and also with named size suffixes (xs, sm, md, lg, xl) for responsive variants.
